title: scorzoneroides carpetana subsp. nevadensis
intro: Scorzoneroides carpetana subsp. nevadensis, also known as the Western scorpion grass or Spanish broom, is a plant species belonging to the genus Scorzoneroides. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly in its root, which contains sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and relieve pain. Scorzoneroides carpetana subsp. nevadensis can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or decoction, and is also used in traditional folk medicine in various parts of the world.
